Title: UGT76B1, a promiscuous hub of small molecule-based immune signaling, glucosylates N-hydroxypipecolic acid, and balances plant immunity

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key immune signal required for systemic acquired resistance and activation of SA-dependent defense
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, including local and distal systemic tissue after pathogen attack
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for NHP biosynthesis and for the constitutive SAR-like resistance of ugt76b1; introgression of fmo1 abolishes NHP accumulation, reduces SA pathway activation, and suppresses enhanced resistance of ugt76b1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic double-mutant analysis (fmo1 ugt76b1); pathogen resistance and SAR assays; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FMO1 is required for NHP biosynthesis and thus for formation of both NHP-H1 and NHP-H2. 2. Loss of FMO1 abolishes constitutive NHP accumulation and enhanced resistance in ugt76b1. 3. FMO1 expression is induced by exogenous ILA and constitutively elevated in ugt76b1.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by pathogen attack, Pip/defense activation, and exogenous ILA; constitutively elevated in ugt76b1
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae infection; exogenous ILA; exogenous Pip
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss compromises SAR and basal resistance
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong positive lever for SAR activation, but functional SA pathway is also required; suitable for resistance engineering with caution for immune-growth balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although FMO1 is a pathway enzyme upstream of NHP, in this paper it mainly serves as genetic evidence establishing NHP dependence of the UGT76B1 phenotype

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase (UGT); plant secondary product glycosyltransferase fold
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: N-hydroxypipecolic acid, salicylic acid, and isoleucic acid glucosylation in plant immune metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: O-β-glucoside formation branch of NHP and SA metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely inactive/homeostatic conjugated form that attenuates immune signaling and balances basal immunity and SAR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, including locally infected and distal systemic leaves
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: N-hydroxypipecolic acid; salicylic acid; isoleucic acid; UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the hydroxyl group of N-hydroxypipecolic acid, salicylic acid, and isoleucic acid to form the respective O-β-glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point/homeostatic hub for active NHP and SA pools
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes NHP-H2 formation and restrains free NHP and SA accumulation; loss of function elevates NHP, SA, Pip, and ILA and causes constitutive SAR-like resistance, whereas overexpression shifts pools toward O-β-glucosides and abolishes SAR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; overexpression analysis; recombinant enzyme assay in vitro with UDP-glucose; LC-MS and GC-MS metabolite profiling; isotope feeding with D9-NHP/D9-Pip; substrate competition assays; pathogen resistance and SAR assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT76B1 is necessary for in vivo formation of the pathogen-, BTH-, and Pip-induced NHP hexoside NHP-H2. 2. Recombinant UGT76B1 directly glucosylates NHP in vitro, and the product matches plant NHP-H2. 3. UGT76B1 also glucosylates SA and ILA, and these three substrates competitively interfere with each other. 4. Loss of UGT76B1 elevates active NHP and SA and establishes constitutive SAR-like resistance. 5. Overexpression of UGT76B1 shifts NHP and SA pools to O-β-glucosides and compromises basal immunity and SAR.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Pip, systemic SAR, pathogen infection, and enhanced by exogenous SA; slightly induced by ILA; repressed in sid2 background
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced during defense activation and SAR-associated conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pseudomonas syringae infection; benzothiadiazole treatment; exogenous pipecolic acid; exogenous isoleucic acid
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive overexpression in UGT76B1-OE line; specific promoter not stated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ugt76b1 shows slightly reduced rosette size; constitutive overexpression lowers basal immunity and abolishes SAR
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful immune homeostasis node, but editing must balance resistance and growth: reducing UGT76B1 can enhance resistance at growth cost, whereas overexpression is detrimental to SAR; best considered for fine-tuning rather than strong constitutive overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT76B1-dependent NHP-H2 is distinguished from UGT76B1-independent NHP-H1, which is likely an NHP glucose ester; UGT76B1 forms O-β-glucosides, not glucose esters
